Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

Add complete F4-F7 results: linear probe, scaled positions, adversarial tests

- F4: Linear probe on raw activations achieves 90-100%, matching oracle at L75%
  SAE encoding does not improve over raw activations
- F5: Position sensitivity more gradual than initially reported (>=5 for reliable)
- F6: Adversarial tests confirm oracle reads activations (83% counterfactual, 100% negation)
- F7: Oracle 100% at N=50 (CI [93-100%]) across all layers
- Updated writeup with all results, revised discussion and recommendations

commit.message:
Add Resend email notifications + join opt-in + privacy policy update (#43) (#46)

* Add disband notification popup and merge ToS/Privacy checkbox (#42)

* UX: notes truncation, creator leave flow, default seats 4, PST admin

- my_party.html: truncate notes at 15 chars with a "See more" popup modal
- my_party.html: creator leaving with other members shows a transfer
  ownership modal (pick new owner); creator leaving alone shows a
  confirmation then deletes the party via existing leave endpoint
- app.py: new POST /api/carpools/<id>/transfer-and-leave endpoint
  to transfer ownership to a chosen member and remove the creator
- create_a_carpool.html + app.py: default seats changed from 3 → 4
- app.py: to_pst Jinja2 filter (UTC-8) for admin panel timestamps
- admin.html: created_at column now shown in PST via the to_pst filter



* Add disband notification popup and combine ToS/Privacy into one checkbox

- New static/notifications.js: modal popup shown on page load when user
  has unread notifications (e.g. party disbanded by creator). Each
  notification has a Dismiss button. Popup injected into body on all
  authenticated pages (start_now, find_a_carpool, create_a_carpool,
  my_party). Replaces the banner-based notification in my_party.html.
- login.html + auth.js: merged EULA/ToS and Privacy Policy into a single
  checkbox ("I agree to the Terms of Service & EULA and Privacy Policy")



---------



* Add Twilio SMS notifications + SMS opt-in on join + privacy policy update

- Add twilio package to requirements.txt
- Add TWILIO_* env vars to .env.example
- Add sms_opt_in column to users table (CREATE TABLE + ensure_columns migration)
- Add send_sms() helper (lazy import, no-ops if Twilio unconfigured)
- Add notify_user() helper combining in-app notification + conditional SMS
- Hook notify_user() into join, remove-member, transfer-and-leave, and disband endpoints
- Add SMS opt-in checkbox (checked by default) to join party modal in search.js
- Include sms_opt_in in join POST body; persist to users table on join
- Update privacy policy: explicit user profile data list, SMS use, Twilio third-party entry, updated date



* Replace Twilio SMS with free SMTP email notifications

- Remove twilio from requirements.txt (stdlib smtplib used instead)
- Replace TWILIO_* env vars with SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD/FROM in .env.example
- Rename sms_opt_in → email_notif_opt_in column throughout (schema, migrations, queries)
- Replace send_sms() with send_email_notification() using smtplib + STARTTLS
- notify_user() now sends directly to user email — no phone lookup needed
- Update join modal checkbox label: "Send me email updates for this carpool"
- Update privacy policy: SMS → email, remove Twilio third-party entry



* Switch email notifications from SMTP to Resend API

- Replace SMTP_* config with RESEND_API_KEY + RESEND_FROM
- Rewrite send_email_notification() using urllib.request (already imported) —
  single POST to api.resend.com/emails, no new packages needed
- Default RESEND_FROM to onboarding@resend.dev for zero-config testing
- Update .env.example and privacy policy to reference Resend



* Remove SerpApi flight lookup (unused, paid service)

- Delete SERPAPI_API_KEY/ENDPOINT/TIMEOUT config vars
- Delete AIRLINE_HUBS dict and _build_hub_pairs()
- Delete _serpapi_search_flights(), _extract_flights_from_serpapi(),
  _lookup_present_or_future_flights(), _suggest_flights_for_airline(),
  _fetch_flight_live_or_future()
- Remove /api/flights/suggest endpoint
- Remove serpapi_key_set from health check
- Remove unused urllib.parse imports (quote, urlencode)
- Remove SERPAPI_API_KEY from .env.example

Airline code autocomplete (/api/airlines/suggest) is unaffected —
it uses the hardcoded AIRLINE_CODES dict, not SerpApi.



---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Show total pieces not boxes in Total Qty column

Parses pack size from description like "(500/Box)" or "(25/Bag)"
and multiplies qty × pack size to show actual piece count.
e.g. 2 Boxes of (500/Box) screws shows "1,000 Screws"
Tooltip shows full breakdown: "2 BOX × 500/Box = 1,000 Screws"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix broken nav subcategory links: rename letter-prefixed pages

Pages with letter prefixes (A_, B_, C_, D_, E_) were not routed
correctly by Streamlit — the prefix was not stripped, causing 404s
for all Quant dropdown links and the Screener link.

Renamed to double-digit numeric prefixes (10–14) so Streamlit
strips the prefix and routes correctly:
  A_Optimize   → 10_Optimize   → /Optimize
  B_Options    → 11_Options    → /Options
  C_MonteCarlo → 12_MonteCarlo → /MonteCarlo
  D_Seasonality → 13_Seasonality → /Seasonality
  E_Screener   → 14_Screener   → /Screener

Nav links in lib/nav.py are unchanged.

Make textarea uncontrolled to eliminate input lag (#558)

Every keystroke previously triggered a full Yew re-render of the
entire SessionView component tree (messages, tasks sidebar, etc).

Now the textarea is uncontrolled — no value= binding. The browser
handles input natively with zero re-renders. A set_input_text()
helper writes directly to the DOM for history navigation, voice
transcription, and send/clear operations. get_input_text() reads
from the DOM at send time.

commit.message:
Fix quiz answer race condition + add debug logging for quiz flow

Two fixes:
1. Guard all QuizManager answer handlers against overriding
   currentScreen='diveResults' that was set by endDive() when O2
   depletes during resume (synchronous event chain race condition)
2. Add [QM] and [App] debug logging to trace quiz answer flow

The O2 depletion race: when a wrong quiz answer's O2 penalty (or
consistency penalty) depletes oxygen, endDive() runs synchronously
via the 'oxygen-depleted' event, setting currentScreen='diveResults'
and stopping MineScene. QuizManager then unconditionally overrode
this with currentScreen='mining', leaving the player on a blank
stopped scene.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial Flask app for Federal Register claims search

Replaces the PHP federal_register-search.php with a modern Flask interface
querying the same PostgreSQL database. Includes:
- Individual claim pages with linked BLM patents, trust-fee conversions, and PLSS parcels
- Tribe landing pages with summary stats, timeline charts, and sortable claims tables
- Master search with name/tribe/allotment/date/claim-type filters (server-side DataTables)
- Timeline visualization showing forced fee patents by year
- About page with policy era context
- CSV download for filtered results and per-tribe exports
- GLO record links with correct docClass codes (SER, MV, IF, STA, IA)
- Linkified patent references in remarks fields
- 10,976 claims: 9,649 forced fee patents + 1,327 secretarial transfers

feat: demote near-duplicate GCPs to end of sorted list

Add _gcp_distance_m() and _separate_near_duplicates() helpers.
Before structural sorting, GCPs within --dup-tolerance metres of a
higher-priority GCP are moved to a demoted bucket and appended after the
sorted list (or omitted entirely with --omit-duplicates).

Default tolerance: 1.0 m. Set to 0 to disable.

Fixes GCP 27 being hidden on map by GCP 90 (0.014 m apart on same
physical target). In ghostrider gulch data this demotes 25 near-duplicate
GCPs, keeping the top-7 slots clean.

commit.message:
fix: add MCP initialize handshake before tools/call

The remote MCP server requires initialize before accepting tools/call.
Without it, tool calls return an error immediately — scrapers fail fast,
appear at the top of the progress list, and show X instead of checkmark.

Also threads the Mcp-Session-Id header for stateful servers, and
improves response parsing to handle both batch and single responses.

recs exist, and caps all results to 7 days out.

commit.message:
fix(chart): auto-select first asset and always mark candlesReady on load

Two bugs caused the chart to hang until the user explicitly clicked a stock:

1. fetchCandlesForAsset returned early on !res.ok, never dispatching
   candlesSeeded, so candlesReady[symbol] stayed false forever.
   Now always dispatches with empty arrays as fallback so the gate clears.

2. selectedAsset started as null; chart had no symbol to render.
   Now auto-selects data[0].symbol immediately after assets load, so
   the chart (and market depth) pre-populate on first paint.

commit.message:
feat: add strikePrice to markets — fix resolution logic (price >= strikePrice → YES)

Markets always resolved YES because `outcomeYes = pending.price > 0` compared
against zero instead of a meaningful threshold. Added `int64 strikePrice` to
MarketMeta, createMarket signature, and MarketCreated event. PythResolver now
reads strikePrice from the factory and uses `pending.price >= strikePrice`.

fix: wire Jasper create_video to AI script generation service

Jasper's create_video tool was using its own hardcoded templates
("Did you know...") instead of the AI scriptwriter. Extracted all
script generation logic into a shared service used by both the
decompose API route and Jasper's tool.

- New: src/lib/video/script-generation-service.ts (single source of truth)
- Jasper create_video now calls generateVideoScripts() with Brand DNA
- Removed ContentManager → Video Specialist template pipeline from create_video
- decompose/route.ts now delegates to the shared service
- Both paths get AI-written scripts with multi-engine direction
